{"text": "[[File:AbilityTokens.png|thumb|300px|A List of Ability Tokens,]]
        '''Ability Tokens''' are tokens generated by [[bees]] which can be collected to activate
        special abilities and buffs. Almost all bees spawn ability tokens, with the exceptions being
        ungifted [[Basic Bee]] and [[Brave Bee]] without [[beequip]]s. If the player doesn't collect
        a token within a certain amount of time, it fades away. Players can't collect the tokens
        generated by other players' bees and vice versa, as the tokens are client sided. Prior to
        the [[Updates#2018.05.07|May 7th, 2018 update]], other players' tokens were visible, but
        still couldn't be collected by others.

        [[File:Focus Bee token.gif|thumb|A [[Looker Bee]] generating a focus token.]]
        Ability tokens can be generated in 3 situations:
        *'''Gathering:''' Ability tokens that can be generated when the bees are collecting
        [[pollen]]. Most ability tokens that can be generated under this situation can collect
        pollen and/or boost pollen collection.
        *'''Combat:''' Ability tokens that can be generated when the bees are targeting/attacking
        [[Mobs|mob]]. Most ability tokens that can be generated under this situation can inflict
        damage or [[Buffs & Debuffs|debuffs]] to enemies and boost bees' combat process.
        *'''Idle:''' Ability tokens that can be generated when neither the bees are collecting
        pollen or in combat situation.
        Some ability tokens can be generated under 2 or 3 different situations. In common cases are
        ability tokens that can be generating in gathering and combat situations, or known as
        \"active\" situations.

        Some tokens aren't generated by the player's bees (such as honey tokens from flowers). The
        player can tell apart the two types of tokens by the fact that tokens dropped by bees give
        off gold sparkles, while tokens generated through other means do not sparkle. Also, tokens
        dropped by someone else's bees are not visible to the player, which means every visible
        token is collectible.

        Each ability token has a use cooldown, a trial cooldown and a trial success rate. When a bee
        reaches a flower, it checks if its use cooldown for each token is off. When its use cooldown
        for a token is off, the bee will check if its trial cooldown for the token is off every time
        it reaches a flower, and attempts to summon the token if it can. If it successfully summons
        the token, it goes back into its use cooldown.

        __TOC__
        ==Ability Token Stats==
        These are stats which affect the characteristics of ability tokens.
        ===Bee Ability Rate===
        Bee Ability Rate determines the increment or decrement of use cooldown and trial cooldown of
        bees' ability tokens, by dividing the original cooldown and trial cooldown. For example: a
        200% bee ability rate will reduces the cooldown to 50% of the original cooldown (100% ÷
        200%).

        ===Ability Token Lifespan===
        Ability Token Lifespan extends the duration of uncollected ability tokens generated by bees
        before they despawn. It can be boosted by a [[Gifted Bee|Gifted]] [[Stubborn Bee]] and [[Cog
        Amulet]] and by the [[Paper Angel]] and [[Paperclip]] beequips. The maximum amount currently
        possible is 144%.

        ===Ability Duplication Chance===
        [[File:Dupe token.gif|thumb|An ability token being duplicated and that token being
        collected]]
        When there is a chance, ability tokens will spawn its duplicated/duped variants randomly on
        a field. Duped tokens hover over the field and last for double the tokens' lifespan, and
        that lifespan is furtherly increased by 10% per [[Digital Bee|Digital Bee's]] level. Duped
        tokens can be collected/activated by standing beneath it for 1 second.

        Ability duplication chance can be increased by [[Buffs & Debuffs|field corruption]] from
        Digital Bee's [[#Glitch|Glitch]] and [[#Map Corruption|Map Corruption]] ability tokens. It
        also can be be increased from [[Gifted Bee|Gifted]] Digital Bee. Other than from Digital
        Bees, ability duplication chance can also be increased by the 'Corrupt' upgrade purchasable
        from the [[Robo Bear Challenge]] and only applies during that challenge. It can also be
        provided by [[Science Bear]] during his \"Corrupting The Glitched Drive\" quest.

        {{clear}}
        ==Bee Ability Tokens==
        ===Bomb===
        [[File:AllBombs.png|left|thumb|203px|'''Red Bomb''' (left), '''Blue Bomb''' (middle), and
        '''Buzz Bomb '''(right).]]
        '''Bomb''' collects pollen from flowers in an area. '''Buzz Bombs''' collect 7 pollen from
        all types of flowers. '''Red Bombs''' and '''Blue Bombs''' collect 10 pollen from only
        matching-color flowers. The amount of pollen collected increases by 10% per level (e.g.,
        200% on a level 20 bee). Normal Bomb tokens collect from 13 surrounding flowers while Bomb+
        tokens collect from 29 surrounding flowers. This is increased by 1.25x a stack (Up to 10
        stacks for 2.5x bomb pollen). It is to be noted that any and all bombs count towards the
        stack.

        Bomb tokens can be collected to increase their power. Collecting multiple tokens increases
        Bomb power without regard to size or color; however, the color limitations still are
        prevalent.

        Red Bomb is granted by [[Rascal Bee|Rascal]], [[Demon Bee|Demon]], and [[Shy Bee|Shy]] Bees.
        Red Bomb+ is granted by [[Fire Bee|Fire]], [[Demon Bee|Demon]], and Festive Bees. Blue Bomb
        is granted by [[Bumble Bee|Bumble]], [[Bubble Bee|Bubble]], and [[Buoyant Bee|Buoyant]]
        Bees. Blue Bomb+ is granted by [[Frosty Bee|Frosty]], [[Ninja Bee|Ninja]], and [[Vicious
        Bee|Vicious]] Bees. Buzz Bomb is granted by [[Bomber Bee|Bomber]], [[Commander
        Bee|Commander]], and [[Exhausted Bee|Exhausted]] Bees. Buzz Bomb+ is granted by [[Demo
        Bee|Demo]], [[Lion Bee|Lion]], and [[Fuzzy Bee|Fuzzy]] Bees. Balloons during a surprise
        party also have a chance to generate a Blue and a Buzz Bomb token.

        If [[#Bomb Sync|Red Bomb Sync]] is on, all red Bombs collect white pollen. If [[#Bomb
        Sync|Blue Bomb Sync]] is on, all blue Bombs collect white pollen. If both Syncs are active,
        all Bombs collect any color of pollen.

        When [[#Pollen Haze|Pollen Haze]] is active, all Bomb ability tokens are automatically
        collected, and they may also [[Pollination|pollinate]] flower patches that they collect
        from.

        All types of Bomb tokens take 4 seconds to fade away and are only generated while collecting
        pollen. The stacking time (the amount of time a player has to collect another Bomb token for
        its effects to be combined with the previous Bomb) is 5 seconds.

        {{clear}}
        ===Boost===
        [[File:RedBlueBoost.png|left|thumb|185px|'''Red Boost''' (left) and '''Blue Boost'''
        (right).]] [[File:White Boost Token.png|thumb|90x90px|'''White Boost.''']]'''Boost''' grants
        x1.2 pollen per stack from matching-color flowers for 15 seconds. Each color can stack up to
        10 times. The pollen boost applies to the flowers themselves, so it benefits both the player
        and their bees. When collected, red/blue (depending on the type) and white sparkles appear
        on the player's bees and their character.

        Red Boost is granted by [[Rad Bee|Rad]], [[Riley Bee|Riley]], and [[Shy Bee|Shy]] Bees, also
        by [[Festive Bee|Festive Bee's]] [[#Festive Gift|Festive Gift]] and [[#Festive Mark|Festive
        Mark]] abilities, and by unactivated targets from [[Precise Bee|Precise Bee's]] [[#Target
        Practice|Target Practice]] ability. Blue Boost is granted by [[Frosty Bee|Frosty]], [[Cool
        Bee|Cool]], [[Bucko Bee|Bucko]], [[Diamond Bee|Diamond]], and [[Tadpole Bee|Tadpole]] Bees.
        White Boost is granted by [[Windy Bee]]. White Boost and Blue Boost tokens can also be
        generated by balloons during a [[#Surprise Party|Surprise Party]].

        Sometimes flowers generate Boost tokens instead of Honey tokens. These tokens look almost
        identical to Boost tokens generated by bees, except the latter have gold sparkles while the
        former does not. Boost tokens from flowers take more time to fade away. These Boost tokens
        generated by flowers do not count for Ability Token quests and badges. Token Links collect
        Boost tokens generated by the player's bees, but not tokens spawned by the field.

        Boost tokens produced by bees take 4 seconds to fade away and are only generated while
        collecting pollen. The stacking time is the same as the effect duration, 15 seconds. Adding
        another token resets the timer to 15 seconds, i.e. it does not add another 15 seconds.
        {{Clear}}
        ===Haste===
        [[File:Haste Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Haste Image]]
        [[File:F5099004-9E64-45E4-86F6-C471EB506FCF.jpeg|thumb|102px|A ''Haste+'' effect (left) and
        ''Haste'' effect (right).]]
        '''Haste''' grants +10% player movement speed for 20 seconds. It can be stacked up to 10
        times, granting 2x (not +100%). Haste does not affect the speed of bees or of players while
        using the [[Parachute]] or [[Glider]]. The [[Honey Dispenser]], the [[Treat Dispenser]], the
        [[Blueberry Dispenser]], and the [[Strawberry Dispenser]] grant x5 Haste. The [[Royal Jelly
        Dispenser]] grants x10 Haste.

        The Haste buff is consumed by the player's Tornado ([[#Tornado|see below]]) to improve the
        summoned tornado.

        Haste is granted by [[Hasty Bee|Hasty]], [[Shocked Bee|Shocked]], [[Ninja Bee|Ninja]], and
        [[Photon Bee|Photon]] Bees. It can also be granted by bees that equip a [[Toy Drum|toy
        drum]] [[beequip]], a [[Whistle]], and balloons during a surprise
        party.[[File:Capture818218328657923564764.JPG|thumb|139px|Ninja Bee with a Haste Token.]]
        Haste tokens have a use cooldown of 4.5 seconds, a trial cooldown of 4.5 seconds and a trial
        success rate of 25%. It takes 4 seconds to fade away and can be generated at any time. The
        stacking time is the same as the effect duration, 20 seconds. Adding another token resets
        the timer to 20 seconds, i.e. it does not add another 20 seconds.

        Some [[codes]] have given the '''Haste+''' ability, which doubles movement speed. This was
        originally planned as one of Ninja Bee's abilities but was abandoned as it was too
        overpowered. The only area that gives out the Haste+ Ability (excludes codes) is the Free
        Royal Jelly Dispenser inside the [[Star Hall]]. This type of Haste also stacks with the
        normal Haste tokens produced by the bees and given by dispensers which grant up to x4 speed.
        Haste+ lasts for 1 minute.

        The [[Stick Bug Challenge#Splinter Trap|Splinter Trap]] from the [[Stick Bug Challenge]] has
        the same effect icon as Haste+, but instead of getting a speed boost, the player takes 3
        damage every second while also receiving a quarter reduction in movement speed and jump
        power. The player receives a Splinter Trap when either the Stick Bug jumps into the flowers,
        when Stick Bug moves to another field, or anytime when Stick Bug reaches level 12. Instead
        of having a duration, the player must collect pollen to remove the Splinter Trap or
        eventually die.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Haste sound-effect.mp3]]
        {{clear}}

        ===Honey Gift===
        [[File:Honey Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Honey Gift image]]
        [[File:HoneyGift.png|thumb|A Honey Gift token, which can be spawned by either a Honey Bee or
        Diamond Bee|alt=|181x181px]]
        [[Honey Bee]] occasionally generates a '''Honey Gift''' token worth 100 [[honey]] multiplied
        by bee [[Bond|level]] squared. [[Diamond Bee]] also sometimes generates a '''Honey Gift+'''
        token worth 250 honey multiplied by bee level squared. These amounts '''cannot''' be
        increased by [[Loot Luck]] and they depend on the player's [[System Page#Honey From
        Tokens|Honey from Tokens]] statistic. (There is no visual difference between Honey Gift and
        Honey Gift+ tokens).

        Honey tokens take 10 seconds to fade away. Honey Gift tokens (i.e., those dropped by bees)
        can be generated anytime.

        {{clear}}

        ===Focus===
        [[File:Focus Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Focus Token image.]]'''Focus''' grants +3% [[System
        Page#Critical Chance|Critical Chance]] for 20 seconds. It is activated only while the player
        is collecting pollen or fighting. It can be stacked up to 10 times. Critical hits increase
        pollen and damage by 100%. When collected, green and white sparkles appear on the player's
        bees and their character.

        Focus is granted by [[Looker Bee|Looker]], [[Commander Bee|Commander]], and [[Music
        Bee|Music]] Bees. It can also be granted by [[Puppy Bee]] if it equips a [[Reindeer
        Antlers|reindeer antlers]] [[beequip]]. Other than from bees, Focus can be granted by
        activated targets from [[Precise Bee|Precise Bee's]] [[#Target Practice|Target Practice]]
        ability, and the buff can be granted with 80% chance from collecting a Target Practice token
        outside of a field.

        Focus tokens take 4 seconds to fade away and can only be generated by active bees. The
        stacking time is the same as the effect duration, 20 seconds. Adding another token resets
        the timer to 20 seconds, i.e. it does not add another 20 seconds.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Focus.ogg]]

        Note: The audio in-game in sped up and the pitch is increased.

        {{clear}}
        ===Token Link===
        [[File:Token Link Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Token Link image.]]
        A '''Token Link''' collects all other ability tokens (except for other Token Links) and
        grants 25 honey and an additional 10 honey per level for each other token collected this way
        (e.g. 175 honey per token collected on level 15 bee).

        For stacking purposes, note that the ability tokens created first, get used first. If two or
        more link tokens are collected at the same time, they may link the same tokens. The other
        tokens activate once, but the player gets two sets of honey from link tokens.

        Token Link tokens take 4 seconds to fade away and are only generated by active bees.

        Token Link are generated by [[Exhausted Bee|Exhausted]], [[Rage Bee|Rage]], [[Shocked
        Bee|Shocked]], and [[Music Bee|Music]] Bees. It can also be generated by bees that have the
        [[Paper Angel]] [[beequip]] equipped and balloons during a surprise party.

        A Token Link can also collect Honey Gift tokens generated by [[Honey Bee]], [[Diamond Bee]],
        honey tokens from [[Vicious Bee|Vicious Bee's]] Impale, [[Treat|treat]] tokens from [[Puppy
        Bee|Puppy Bee's]] Fetch ability, tokens from [[Festive Bee|Festive Bee's]] Festive Gift
        ability, tokens from Cub Buddy gifts, or tokens from mobs. However, it does not collect
        honey tokens generated by [[Passive Abilities#Coin Scatter|Coin Scatter]], [[Passive
        Abilities|Gummy Star]] or Target Practice.

        {{clear}}
        ===Mark===
        [[File:Marks Token Repicture2.png|thumb|left|185px|'''Pollen Mark''' (left) and '''Honey
        Mark''' (right).]]
        A '''Mark''' token deploys a Mark in a random area on a field that grants a specific buff
        when the player stands in its range. '''Pollen Mark''' increases all pollen by 50% (+50% per
        stack). The buff can stack with other pollen boosting buffs such as Baby Love, Bear Morph,
        and Inspire. '''Honey Mark''' grants 2 conversion links (lets 2 bees convert from your
        backpack) and x1.25 convert rate (+2 and x1.25 per stack respectively). '''Precise Mark'''
        increases Critical Chance and Super-Crit chance by 7%. A normal Mark lasts for 7 seconds
        (+0.2s per level) with a 4-tile-radius range (The mark is activated if the player stands
        within 4 tiles of it.) while Mark+ lasts for 8 seconds (+0.25s per level) with a
        5-tile-radius range. When the player stands in a Mark range, the Mark glows and the light on
        top turns green.[[File:PollenMark.png|thumb|200px|A Pollen Mark on a field.]]

        Each Mark can be stacked to increase its effect, up to 3 stacks. Marks can only stack if two
        or more Marks are linked. Marks can be linked by overlapping each other. If the player
        stands in a Mark that is linked to another one, all linked Marks are activated.

        When the player walks off the Honey Mark's range, conversion links remain and bees still
        convert honey normally until they finish converting.[[File:Linked Pollen
        Mark.png|thumb|200px|Two Pollen Marks stacked above each other.]]

        Pollen Mark is granted by [[Stubborn Bee|Stubborn]] and [[Carpenter Bee|Carpenter]] Bees.
        Pollen Mark+ is granted by [[Vector Bee]]. Honey Mark is granted by [[Honey Bee|Honey]] and
        [[Festive Bee|Festive]] Bees. Honey Mark+ is granted by [[Carpenter Bee]]. It can also be
        generated by balloons during a surprise party.

        The player can see other people's Marks, but they can't see those Marks' range, the Marks
        don't grant buffs to them, they can't be linked with their Marks, and vice versa (except for
        festive marks).

        Connecting 10 marks in a row gives the Saturn [[sticker]].

        All Mark token types take 8 seconds to fade away and are only generated while collecting
        pollen.

        The following audio plays when a mark pops up:[[File:Marktoken.ogg]]

        {{clear}}
        ===Rage===
        [[File:Rage Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Rage Token Image]]
        [[Rage Bee]] and [[Spicy Bee]] generate '''Rage''' tokens that grant bees +1 [[System
        Page#Bee Attack|attack]] for 45 seconds, which can be stacked up to 4 times. When collected,
        an angry war scream can be heard, which happens in increasing pitch with higher stacks of
        Rage.

        Rage tokens take 24 seconds to fade away and are only generated while in combat. The
        stacking time is the same as the effect duration, 45 seconds. Adding another token resets
        the timer to 45 seconds, i.e. it does not add another 45 seconds.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected [[File:RageAbilitySound.ogg]]

        Note: The audio in-game is sped up and the pitch is increased.{{clear}}
        ===<span id=\"Baby Love\"></span>Baby Love===
        [[File:Baby Love Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Baby token Image]]
        [[Baby Bee]] and [[Tadpole Bee|Gifted Tadpole Bee]] generate '''Baby Love''' tokens that
        grant x2 pollen and +50% [[Loot Luck]] for 30 seconds. Loot Luck increases the player's
        chances of getting items from mobs. When collected, a baby's laugh can be heard, and pink
        and white sparkles appear on the player's character. Baby Love does not stack, though
        collecting another Baby Love token resets the timer to 30 seconds. The doubled pollen
        ''does'' stack with the Bear Morph, Inspire, and Pollen Marks.

        Baby Love tokens take 8 seconds to fade away and can be generated at any time.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected: [[File:Giggle.ogg]]

        Note: The audio in-game is sped up and the pitch is increased.

        {{clear}}
        ===Melody===
        [[File:Melody Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Melody token image]]
        [[Music Bee]] generates a '''Melody''' token that grants +100% [[Critical Power]] for 30
        seconds. Critical Power increases the pollen and damage of critical hits. When collected,
        black and white sparkles appear on the player's bees and their character. Melody can also be
        generated by [[#Surprise Party|Surprise Party]]. Certain bees can generate Melody if they
        have a [[Toy Horn|toy horn beequip]] or a [[Kazoo|kazoo beequip]].

        Melody does not stack, but collecting a second Melody token while the Melody is still active
        resets the timer to 30 seconds.

        Melody tokens take 8 seconds to fade away and are only generated by active bees.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Melody.ogg]]

        {{Clear}}

        ===Inflate Balloons===
        [[File:Inflate balloon.png|left|thumb|90px|Inflate Token image]]
        [[Buoyant Bee]] generates an '''Inflate Balloons''' token. When collected, Buoyant Bee
        increases the pollen stored inside the [[Balloon]]s on the field you are in by +5%, which
        increases by +0.01% per level. It then summons a Blue Balloon that floats around the field
        for 20 seconds (+2s per level) with capacity that scales with the player's capacity, this
        bee's level, and currently active [[field boost]] stacks. Pollen collected from flowers
        within the balloon area (shaded flowers) is added to the balloon instead of the container.
        Upon reaching max capacity or reaching the time limit, the balloon floats back to the
        player's hive. Converting from a balloon at the hive grants bonus Capacity and Honey at Hive
        for 1 hour.

        Standing in a balloon aura grants x1.02 Pollen and +2% Honey from Tokens which stacks up to
        10 times.

        If Buoyant Bee is [[Gifted Bee|gifted]], there is a 5% (+0.5% per lvl), doubled if the field
        is boosted, chance to summon a Gold Balloon instead. Gold Balloons have x1.25 capacity of
        blue Balloon, gain bonus pollen, and will float to nearby Bubbles to transform them into
        Gold Bubbles, which collect 50% more pollen and have a chance to spawn Honey Tokens.

        Inflate Balloons tokens take 4 seconds to fade away and are only generated when collecting
        pollen.

        The following audio plays when the token is picked up:

        [[File:Bububub.mp3|left|thumb]]

        {{Clear}}

        ===Surprise Party===
        [[File:Surprise Party Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Surprise Party token image]]
        [[Buoyant Bee|Gifted Buoyant Bee]] generates Surprise Party tokens that summons a Gold
        Balloon (+1 every 5 lvls) for all players, then causes all Balloons to spawn random ability
        tokens for a few seconds. Gold Balloons gain bonus pollen and transform nearby Bubbles into
        Gold Bubbles, which collect 50% more pollen and have a chance to spawn Honey Tokens. Once a
        player collects the token, a big party popper appears with a server-wide message announces:
        \"🎉Surprise balloons from [Player's display name]!\"

        '''Ability tokens spawned by balloons through Surprise Party include:'''

        *[[#Boost|Blue Boost]]
        *White Boost
        *[[#Bomb|Blue Bomb]]
        *Buzz Bomb
        *[[#Mark|Pollen Mark]]
        *Honey Mark
        *[[#Haste|Haste]]
        *[[#Token Link|Token Link]]
        *[[#Melody|Melody]]

        If you triggered Surprise Party within the last 8 minutes, it instead performs Inflate
        Balloons that guarantees a Gold Balloon. The cooldown only applies to you, meaning that
        after you trigger a Surprise Party, other players can also trigger a Surprise Party before
        your cooldown ends.

        Surprise Party take 4 seconds to fade away and are only generated when collecting pollen.

        The following audio plays when the token is activated:
        [[File:PartyMusic.ogg|left|thumb]]
        <br />

        {{Clear}}

        ===Fuzz Bombs===
        [[File:Fuzz Bombs Token.png|thumb|left|Fuzz Bomb token image]]
        [[File:FuzzyAbility.png|thumb|Summoned fuzz bombs on a field.]]
        [[Fuzzy Bee]] generates a '''Fuzz Bombs''' token which summons 2 fuzz bombs (+1 every 5
        levels). Fuzz bombs wander around the field in a zigzag path on which they are spawned for 8
        seconds. When touched by players, tornadoes, or [[Petal Wand|Petal Shurikens]], it collects
        pollen from 29 surrounding flowers and may [[Pollination|pollinate]] nearby [[flowers]]. It
        also grants the [[Buffs & Debuffs#From Ability Tokens|bomb combo buff]] which increases
        bomb power. The amount of pollen collected scales with [[System Page#Buzz Bomb Pollen|Buzz
        Bomb multiplier]] and bee level.

        Fuzz Bombs tokens take 4 seconds to fade away and are only generated when collecting pollen.
        Onett said in Discord that Fuzz Bombs used to be called \"Dust Bunnies\".

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Fuzzbombtoken.ogg]]

        The following audio plays when a fuzz bomb is hit:[[File:Fuzzbombhit.ogg]]

        {{Clear}}

        ===Pollen Haze===
        [[File:Pollen Haze Token.png|thumb|left|Pollen Haze token image]]
        [[Fuzzy Bee|Gifted Fuzzy Bee]] generates a '''Pollen Haze''' token that summons a haze over
        the field for 30 seconds. During that time, up to 30 random [[flowers]]
        [[Pollination|pollinate]] every second, flowers grow faster, and Bomb Tokens are
        automatically collected and have a chance to pollinate flowers.

        If there has been a Pollen Haze over the field within the last 3 minutes, it acts as a Fuzz
        Bombs token instead.

        Pollen Haze tokens take 8 seconds to fade away and are only generated when collecting
        pollen.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Pollenhaze.ogg]]

        Note: The audio in-game is slowed down and pitched down a little.

        {{Clear}}

        ===Target Practice===
        [[File:Target Pratice Token.png|left|thumb|Target Practice token image]]
        [[Precise Bee]] generates a '''Target Practice''' token. When collected on a field, it
        causes the bee to fly above the field you're in and face the part of the field it is
        opposite from. Then it projects 3 targets, from the closest to farthest from the bee for a
        few seconds. Walking on and passing the target(s) activates it which then will shortly be
        shot by the bee, spawning a [[#Focus|Focus]] token and collecting pollen from 29 surrounding
        flowers. The amount of pollen collected is equal to x2 Precise Bee's [[Bee Attack|attack]]
        and increased by +10% per level, and with [[Buffs & Debuffs|Flame Heat]] the shot gains
        up to x4 pollen and up to +50% instant conversion. If the targets aren't activated, they
        spawn [[#Boost|red boost]] tokens instead.

        If the player is shot while standing on a target, it collects all ability tokens and
        consumes all Flame Heat to instantly convert pollen from the player's container, with half
        of that turning into 5 honey tokens. The amount of pollen converted is equal to 50% [[System
        Page#Total Convert|total conversion]] + x10 Precise Bee's convert amount and increased by up
        to x10 with consumed Flame Heat and up to x3 with [[field boost]] stacks.

        When all 3 targets are activated, it collects all ability tokens and grants a stack of
        [[Buffs & Debuffs|Precision]], which grants +2% [[System Page#Super-Crit
        Chance|super-crit chance]] for 60 seconds, stacking up to 10 times. Super-crits only occur
        when performing a critical hit, which doubles and instantly converts pollen collected, while
        attack damage is increased by 33% of pollen from a super-crit.

        If Precise Bee is [[Gifted Bee|gifted]], the farthest target appears in purple. When only
        the farthest target is activated, it will summon a Precise Mark for 12 seconds (+0.5s per
        level) with a 5-tile-radius range. Precise Marks grant +7% critical chance and super-crit
        chance that stacks up to 3 times.

        If the player collects a Target Practice token that is outside of a field, they will
        immediately be granted either the Precision or Focus buff. The chances of getting the
        Precision buff are 20%, and 80% for Focus buff.

        Target Practice tokens take 8 seconds before fading away and are only generated by active
        bees.

        The following audio plays when a target is shot:
        [[File:Rec-tab.mp3|left|thumb]]

        {{clear}}

        ===Inferno===
        [[File:Inferno Token.png|thumb|left|Inferno token image]]
        [[Spicy Bee]] generates an '''Inferno''' token, which summons 6 flames in circle form, and
        then summons 2 [[Fire Bee]]s that last for 15 seconds (Increased by 1 second per level of
        Spicy Bee) with levels equal to 2 less than the bee that summoned them. Flames collect
        pollen, deal damage to enemies ignoring defense and critical hits, and grant [[Buffs &
        Debuffs|Flame Heat]] when the player stands nearby.

        If the Spicy Bee is [[gifted Bee|gifted]], each summoned Fire Bee has a 10% (+1% per lvl)
        chance of being gifted.

        Inferno tokens take 4 seconds to fade away and are only generated by active bees.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Inferno1.ogg]]

        {{clear}}

        ===Flame Fuel===
        [[File:Flame Fuel Token.png|thumb|left|Flame Fuel token image]]
        [[Spicy Bee|Gifted Spicy Bee]] generates a '''Flame Fuel''' token that increases flame
        duration by 50% for 15 seconds. During that time, oil will be tossed to every newly created
        flame, causing them to convert 50,000 pollen + 10% of the player's total hive convert. Oil
        will also be tossed to every newly created dark flames even if they were from the same
        flames.

        Flame Fuel tokens take 4 seconds to fade away and are only generated by active bees.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Flamefuel2.ogg]]

        {{clear}}

        ===Summon Frog===
        [[File:Summon Frog Token.png|thumb|left|Summon Frog token image]]
        [[Tadpole Bee]] generates a '''Summon Frog''' token, which summons a [[Frog]] that hops
        around and creates Bubbles. Frogs can collect tokens by using their tongue which also
        creates Bubbles on collected tokens. Bubbles collect pollen from 29 surrounding flowers and
        causes them to replenish. Frogs also attack nearby enemies with an attack equal to 10 times
        the attack of the bee that summoned the frog. Frogs last for 20 seconds, with the lifespan
        increasing by 2 seconds per bee level.

        If the Tadpole Bee is [[gifted Bee|gifted]], Summon Frog has a 10% (+2% per lvl) chance to
        summon a Gifted Frog instead. Gifted frogs have increased tongue range, token gathering,
        attack speed, 50% more attack, have a chance to summon Gold Bubbles, and can hit
        [[Balloon]]s with its tongue to convert pollen from the balloon into honey tokens.

        Summon Frog tokens take 4 seconds to fade away and are only generated by active bees.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Summonfrog.ogg]]

        {{clear}}

        ===Triangulate===
        [[File:Triangulate Token.png|thumb|left|Triangulate token image]]
        [[Vector Bee]] generates a '''Triangulate''' token which draws a triangle between the token,
        the bee, and the player. When the ability activates, Vector Bee moves in a random direction
        without stopping until the ability ends. After 3 seconds, the triangle collects ability
        tokens and 10 pollen from all flowers inside the triangle, with the amount of pollen
        collected increasing by 2 per level. Additionally, any marks inside the triangle boosts the
        pollen collected by Triangulate by 50%. If the triangle contains at least 1 pollen mark it
        gains x2 white pollen, if it contains at least 1 honey mark or festive mark it gains 50%
        instant conversion, and if it contains at least 1 precise mark it always lands a [[Critical
        Hits|critical hit]].

        Triangulate tokens take 4 seconds to fade away and are only generated when collecting
        pollen. If this token is collected while the container is full, the triangle does not
        appear.

        The following audio plays while triangulate is active:[[File:TriangulateActivate2.ogg]]
        {{Clear}}

        ===Mark Surge===
        [[File:Mark Surge Token.png|thumb|left|Mark Surge token image]]
        [[Vector Bee#Gifted|Gifted Vector Bee]] generates a '''Mark Surge''' token which causes all
        active marks to collect 7 pollen from all flowers inside their radius, with the amount of
        pollen collected increased by 10% per level. It also extends the duration of all active
        marks by 1 second (increases by 0.1 seconds per level). If the mark is a honey mark or
        festive mark the pollen is instantly converted, and if the mark is precise mark it always
        lands a [[Critical Hits|critical hit]]. Each mark can only perform up to 5 surges.

        Mark Surge tokens take 4 seconds to fade away and are only generated when collecting pollen.
        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Marksurge.ogg]]

        {{clear}}
        ===Inspire===
        [[File:Inspire Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Inspire token
        image]][[File:RobloxScreenShot20180807 120631783.png|thumb|128px|A star that appears when an
        Inspire token is collected.]]
        All [[Gifted Bee]]s can generate an '''Inspire''' token, which grants x2 pollen to the
        player for 5 seconds. The doubled pollen applies to both the player and their bees. This
        token stacks up to 50 times. Each stack increases the multiplier by 1 and resets the timer.
        When collected, a small glowing star appears, floating up from the token while fading away.
        Inspire tokens take 8 seconds to fade away and are only generated while collecting pollen.
        The Inspire buff can stack with Bear Morph, Baby Love, and Pollen Mark.

        Inspire can also be activated by the [[Star Amulet]] passive [[Passive Abilities#Star
        Shower|Star Shower]] which spawns a big star in the field it is activated in. Then green
        spots appear where stars will fall, and when caught by the player, they are granted x1
        Inspire.

        During Beesmas 2020, 2021 and 2022, it is also possible to gain the buff by catching one of
        [[Science Bear]]'s [[Beesmas Lights]], which work similarly to the Star Shower ability.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected. The higher the stack, the higher the
        pitch of the audio is:[[File:Inspiring.ogg]]

        {{clear}}

        ==Event Bee Exclusive Ability Tokens==
        ===Bear Morph===
        [[File:BearMorphs-0.png|left|thumb|254px|Bear morph tokens (Top), Bear morphed by a player
        (Bottom. From left to right: '''Black Bear''', '''Brown Bear''', '''Panda Bear''', '''Polar
        Bear''', '''Science Bear''', and '''Mother Bear.''']]
        [[Bear Bee]] generates a '''Bear Morph''' token that transforms the player into the bear on
        the token. It grants ×2 pollen, sets the player's base movement speed to 24 (up from 18),
        and sets the player's base jump power to 65 (up from 55). The player's base stats are the
        stats they start with before adding boosts from gifted hive bonuses, amulets, abilities,
        etc. The effects of the token do not vary for each type of bear. The transformation lasts 30
        seconds. The Bear Morph cannot transform the player into [[Spirit Bear]], [[Dapper Bear]],
        [[Robo Bear]], [[Sun Bear]], [[Gummy Bear]], [[Noob Bear]], [[Pro Bear]], [[Top Bear]],
        [[Tunnel Bear]], [[Shadow Bear]] or [[Bee Bear]]. However, the player can be transformed
        into a Gummy Bear by using the code \"''HappyNewYear''\"[invalid] or when the [[Gummy Mask]]
        [[Passive Abilities#Gummy Morph|passive ability]] activates.

        If Bear Bee is Gifted, it has a 20% chance to produce a morph for [[Mother Bear]] or
        [[Science Bear]]. ''Mother Bear Morph'' grants ×2.5 pollen, ×1.5 bee gather pollen and also
        sets the player's base movement speed to 24, while ''Science Bear Morph'' grants +1
        Conversion Link as well as setting the player's base movement speed to 24.

        Since the player can only have one Bear Bee in their hive, there's no way for the Bear Morph
        to stack aside from [[Codes|code]] boosts and if the token is duped by a digital bee.
        However, Bear Morph's effects indeed stack if multiple are obtained. The effects also stack
        with other effects such as Baby Love, giving quadruple pollen instead of the usual double.
        Haste and Haste+ also stacks, as Haste/Haste+ with Bear Morph is faster than Haste/Haste+ on
        its own. With the addition of Digital Bee, there is a chance that a Bear Morph token will
        become duplicated when collected under field corruption, therefore making the obtainment of
        multiple bear morph stacks more feasible.
        [[File:Screen Shot 2021-02-01 at 1.10.17 PM.png|thumb|A Bear morph Buff's doubled Panda bear
        Morph buff (Left)<br/>Gummy bear morph (Right)]]
        Bear Morph tokens take 15 seconds to fade away and can be generated at any time.

        Collecting the Bear Morph plays the same sound as collecting a boost token.
        [[File:Screen Shot 2021-03-25 at 6.41.44 PM.png|thumb|When the player leaves and rejoins
        while Bear Morph is active.]]

        There's a glitch that when the player is wearing any gear while Bear Morph is active, they
        can have the Bear Morph on with the gear visible. There's also another glitch that when the
        player leaves the game while bear morph is on then quickly join back while the buff is
        active, their head and left hand fall off.

        If the player dies while Bear Morph is active, the effect is
        lost.[[File:Lolwtf.PNG|left|thumb|220px|When the player equips a hat when Bear Morph is
        active.]]

        {{clear}}

        ===Pulse===
        [[File:Pulse.png|left|thumb|185px|'''Blue Pulse''' (left) and '''Red Pulse''' (right).]]
        '''Pulse''' fires a Pulse that hops between its matching-color bees that are active. '''Blue
        Pulse''' hops between blue bees while '''Red Pulse''' hops between red bees. When the Pulse
        hops between bees, it collects pollen from 9 surrounding flowers around them. Power
        increases with each hop and the amount of pollen collected increases by 10% per level (e.g.
        200% on a level 20 bee).

        Blue Pulse is granted by [[Cobalt Bee]] and Red Pulse is granted by [[Crimson Bee]]. If the
        player activates either Pulse and they own both Cobalt and Crimson Bees, both Red and Blue
        Pulses are released. If this token is Duped, it will only fire a matching-color pulse,
        regardless of if the player owns both bees or not.

        Pulse tokens take 12 seconds to fade away and are only generated while collecting pollen.

        The [[Passive Abilities]] of the [[Cobalt Guard]] (Haste Pulser) and the [[Crimson Guard]]
        (Focus Pulser) can fire a pulse. However, neither fires two Pulses if the player has both of
        the guards.

        {{clear}}
        ===Bomb Sync===
        [[File:Bomb Sync Token.png|left|thumb|185px|'''Blue Bomb Sync''' (left) and '''Red Bomb
        Sync''' (right).]]
        '''Bomb Sync''' allows its matching-color [[#Bombs|Bombs]] to collect pollen from white
        flowers for 30 seconds. '''Blue Bomb Sync''' allows Blue Bombs while '''Red Bomb Sync'''
        allows Red Bombs to collect pollen from white flowers. If both are active, both Blue and Red
        Bombs can collect pollen from all flowers (They act as Buzz Bombs but with better pollen
        collection).

        Blue Bomb Sync is granted by [[Cobalt Bee]], and Red Bomb Sync is granted by [[Crimson
        Bee]].

        Bomb Sync tokens take 24 seconds to fade away and are only generated while collecting
        pollen.

        The description for the Red Bomb Sync boost used to be \"If Red Bomb Sync is active, 🍎 s
        (apples) to blue flowers too.\" This was an Easter Egg after [[Onett (Developer)|Onett]]
        accidentally typed \"apples\" instead of \"applies\" in Red Bomb Sync's description. This was
        later changed to the apple emoji, and then was changed to the proper word \"applies\".
        [[File:Apple.PNG|thumb|289x289px|The Red Bomb Sync's description before the 12/26/21
        update.|alt=]]

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Bsync.ogg]]

        In the Beesmas 2021 update, the apple emoji in the Red Bomb Sync was changed into 'applies'.
        {{clear}}
        ===Festive Gift===
        [[File:Festive Gift Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Festive Gift token image]]
        [[Festive Bee]] generates a '''Festive Gift''' token, which grants random gifts (tokens) to
        all players on the server. These gifts are improved with each level. If Festive Bee is
        Gifted, '''Festive Gifts''' can grant better gifts. The tokens from the gift can be
        collected by Token Links, and the player can't see or collect any other player's gifts. Once
        a player collects the token, a big present appears above all players and a server-wide
        message announces: \"<span style=\"color:white;background:red;\">🎁 Festive Gifts from
        [Player's display name]! 🎁</span>\". [[File:RobloxScreenShot20181221 085210703
        (3).png|126px|right|A player recieving a festive gift from another player.]]
        {|class=\"article-table\"
        !The gifts include
        !If Festive Bee is Gifted
        |-
        |{{Cost|Red Boost Token|||0}}<br>{{Cost|Festive Blessing
        Token|||0}}<br>{{Cost|Treat}}<br>{{Cost|Sunflower
        Seed}}<br>{{Cost|Strawberry}}<br>{{Cost|Pineapple}}<br>{{Cost|Blueberry}}<br>{{Cost|Honey}}<br>{{Cost|Gumdrops}}<br>{{Cost|Royal
        Jelly}}<br>{{Cost|Ticket|||0}} (Rare)<br>{{Cost|Blue Extract|||0}}
        (Rare)<br>{{Cost|Red Extract|||0}} (Rare)<br>{{Cost|Magic Bean|||0}}
        (Rare)<br>{{Cost|Micro-Converter|||0}} (Rare)<br>{{Cost|Festive Bean|||0}}
        (Exceptionally rare)
        |{{Cost|Beesmas Cheer Token|||0}}<br>{{Cost|Glue|||0}}
        (Rare)<br>{{Cost|Oil|||0}} (Rare)<br>{{Cost|Enzymes|||0}}
        (Rare)<br>{{Cost|Glitter|||0}} (Rare)<br>{{Cost|Star Jelly|||0}}
        (Rare)<br>{{Cost|Field Dice|||0}} (Rare)
        |}
        Festive Gift tokens take 24 seconds to fade away, and can be generated at any time while the
        player's in a field. Meanwhile the gifts (tokens) fade away in 8 seconds. It seems to spawn
        rarely, about once every 10 minutes.

        '''NOTE: '''''The buffs from Festive Gift aren't guaranteed to spawn.''

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Dth1.ogg]]

        {{clear}}

        ===Festive Blessing===
        [[File:Festive Blessing Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Festive Blessing token image]]
        '''Festive Blessing''' grants +100% [[System Page#Instant Conversion|Instant Conversion]]
        and x2 [[System Page#Convert Rate|Conversion Rate]] to the player for 10 seconds. It can be
        stacked to increase the timer, capping at 5 minutes. Festive Blessing tokens are granted by
        Festive Gifts and [[Sprout|Festive Sprouts]]. When collecting this token, A message appears:
        \"[Activated \"Festive Blessing\" (Duration: {time}]\"

        This and [[#Beesmas Cheer|Beesmas Cheer]] are one of the few of the ability tokens that add
        to the duration instead of resetting its timer.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Dth2.ogg]]

        {{clear}}
        ===Beesmas Cheer===
        [[File:Beesmas Cheer Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Beesmas Cheer token image]]
        '''Beesmas Cheer''' grants x2 Bee Movespeed and x1.5 Pollen to the player for 10 seconds. It
        can be stacked to increase the timer, capping at 5 minutes. Beesmas Cheer tokens are granted
        by Festive Gifts (if Festive Bee is '''Gifted)''' and Festive Sprouts. When collecting this
        token, A message appears: \"[Activated \"Beesmas Cheer\" (Duration: {time})]\"

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Dth3.ogg]]

        {{clear}}

        ===Glob===
        [[File:Glob Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Glob token image]]
        [[Gummy Bee]] generates a '''Glob''' token that covers 49 surrounding flowers in [[Goo]] and
        81 surrounding flowers when Gifted. Flowers covered in Goo grant bonus Honey that increases
        with the size of the goo puddle. Since the formula for goo bonus is (number of flowers in
        the puddle) * 0.005) ^ 0.5, the Glob bonus (on its own, i.e. if there isn't another source
        of goo in the field) comes to (49*0.005)^0.5 = 0.495, or almost 50%.

        This and Gumdrop Barrage (see below) are also used to activate the [[Gummy Mask|Gummy
        Mask's]] [[Passive Abilities|passive ability]].

        Glob tokens take 8 seconds to fade away and are only generated while collecting pollen.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Glob.ogg]]

        Note: The sound clip above is actually used for Gumdrop Barrage instead. Glob makes a
        splatter noise when collected.

        {{clear}}

        ===Gumdrop Barrage===
        [[File:Gumdrop Barrage Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Gumdrop Barrage token image]]
        [[Gummy Bee]] generates a '''Gumdrop Barrage''' token that launches [[Gumdrops]] in a large
        area, covering the field in [[Goo]]. Flowers covered in Goo grant bonus Honey that increases
        with the size of the Goo puddle.

        Gumdrop Barrage tokens take 8 seconds to fade away and are only generated while collecting
        pollen.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Gbarrage.ogg]]

        Note that the audio in-game has a higher pitch.

        {{clear}}
        ===Beamstorm===
        [[File:Beamstorm Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Beamstorm token image]]
        [[File:BeamStorm.png|thumb|A beamstorm has activated by a player.]]
        [[Photon Bee]] generates a '''Beamstorm''' token that produces 25 beams (+2 per level). Each
        of the beams of light collects all the pollen in 1 square and doubles the pollen collected
        from the flowers they hit. The beams can collect the pollen in a 15x15 tile range.

        If Photon Bee is gifted, the Beamstorm token's beams appear as white and the pollen
        collected by the beams is instantly converted into honey, in addition to white pollen
        collected being triple.

        Beamstorm tokens take 12 seconds to fade away and are only generated when collecting pollen.
        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Bstormbackground.ogg]]

        Note: Ignoring the individual sound of the beams, the audio in-game is slowed down and
        pitched down by ~70%.

        {{clear}}

        ===Puppy Love===
        [[File:Puppy Love Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Puppy Love token image]]
        [[Puppy Bee]] generates a '''Puppy Love''' token that increases the [[bond]] of the nearest
        10 bees by 30 and an additional 10 bond per average level of all bees in hive, and it also
        grants 10% bee movespeed and 50% bee gather amount. When collected, a dog bark can be heard,
        a light is emitted, and a tiny heart appears.

        Puppy Love tokens take 8 seconds to fade away and are generated when collecting pollen or
        when idle.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:
        [[File:Ohwhosagoodboywhoosagoodboy.ogg]]

        {{clear}}
        ===Fetch===
        [[File:Fetch Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Fetch token image]]
        [[Puppy Bee]] generates a '''Fetch''' token. When collected, a ball spawns in front of the
        player. The player must kick the ball by touching it or it despawns in 5 seconds. Then the
        Puppy Bee chases the ball to try and pass it back to the player, despawning in the same
        amount of time. The player and the Puppy Bee can keep passing the ball back to each other
        until it despawns. As the ball is passed over, it can collect pollen, tokens (other than
        treat tokens from Fetch itself), pop fuzz bombs, and bubbles. The amount of pollen collected
        is increased by 100% and an additional 15% per level (e.g. 400% pollen increased on level 20
        bee). Every 3rd kick made by Puppy Bee creates a combo that spawns a treat token at Puppy
        Bee's current location, which increases in value. Unlike other treat tokens, these can be
        collected by Token Link. As the combo continues, the ball moves faster when kicked and it
        collects more pollen until it reaches the max combo that gives 256 treats (9 combos). Any
        further combo won't spawn treat tokens. The player can get a maximum of 512 treats per fetch
        ability.

        [[File:FetchGBB.jpg|thumb|225x225px|Gingerbread Bear + 512 Treats combo.]]
        When Puppy Bee is equipped with the [[Reindeer Antlers|reindeer antlers]] [[beequip]], the
        \"Fetch\" is enhanced to \"Reindeer Fetch\". Puppy's ball glows bright red and emits white
        sparkles, and every combo grants the player \"Reindeer Guidance\" which grants x1.01 capacity
        for 15 minutes and stacks up to 10 times for up to x1.1 capacity. Additionally, the ball has
        a small chance to spawn [[Gingerbread Bear|gingerbread bears]] at high combo ('''max 1 per
        day'''). While 256 treats is normally the highest amount from a combo, it's possible to get
        to 512 treats if the gingerbread bear spawns in the middle of the combo progression.

        The ball can sometimes fall outside of the field, out of range for the player or Puppy Bee
        to continue passing the ball to each other. It also sometimes gets stuck and allows the
        player to 'pass' it easily. While the Puppy Bee plays Fetch, it does not do anything else
        but play Fetch. Playing Fetch can be dangerous if the player's bees are attacking enemies,
        as they can run into an enemy as they chase the ball.

        Fetch tokens take 8 seconds to fade away and are only generated while collecting pollen.

        The following audio plays when the token is
        collected:[[File:Ohwhosagoodboywhoosagoodboy.ogg]]

        The following audio plays when either the player or Puppy Bee has hit the
        ball:[[File:Fetchhit.ogg]]

        Note: The pitch that the ball makes depends on its color (i.e. number of passes until a
        combo is performed) with green being the normal pitch, orange being a higher pitch and red
        being the highest pitch. The pitch of the ball will reset as well as its color after a
        combo.

        {{clear}}

        ===Scratch===
        [[File:Scratch Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Scratch token image]]
        [[Tabby Bee]] generates a '''Scratch''' token that collects 7 pollen from 3 lines of 4
        flowers, the amount of pollen collected increased by 1 per level (e.g. 27 pollen on level 20
        bee). When collecting the token, a cat's growl is heard and three cat claws rip through the
        field around the token. The pollen collected can be increased through Tabby Love, granting
        0.16 pollen per stack.

        If Tabby Bee is Gifted, Scratch always lands critical hits.

        Scratch tokens take 12 seconds to fade away and are only generated while collecting pollen.
        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Scratch.ogg]]

        Note that the audio in-game has a slightly higher pitch.

        {{clear}}
        ===Tabby Love===
        [[File:Tabby Love Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Tabby Love token image]]
        [[Tabby Bee]] generates a '''Tabby Love''' token that permanently improves the stats of a
        player's Tabby Bee. For each stack of Tabby Love, a Tabby Bee collects 1% more pollen,
        converts 1% more pollen, and improves Scratch's pollen gain by 1%. Going by the base
        numbers, this means that the Tabby Bee collects 0.1 more pollen and converts 1.6 more pollen
        per stack of Tabby Love. Tabby Love can be stacked up to 1,000 times.

        Tabby Love tokens take 16 seconds to fade away and are only generated while collecting
        pollen. The tokens seem to spawn much rarer than other tokens but seem to be able to spawn
        when collecting pollen at a rate of about 1 token every 5–15 minutes with an average of 4-7
        tokens being able to be gathered per hour. Tabby Love still spawns after it reaches 1000
        although it does not stack any further. If at max stacks, Tabby Love grants +1% Critical
        Chance and +25% Critical Power for 20 seconds (+2s per lvl). If Gifted, grants +1%
        Super-Crit Chance as well.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Tabluvvv.ogg]]

        Note: The audio in-game is pitched down a little.

        {{clear}}

        ===Impale===
        [[File:Impale Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Impale token image]]
        [[Vicious Bee]] generates an '''Impale''' token that summons spikes equal to the bee's level
        that damages enemies for 5% of their current health and increases with Vicious Bee's attack,
        but the damage is reduced past 1,000. Each enemy hit by a spike generates a honey token
        worth 50 honey plus an additional 50 per bee level. Like Honey Gift, these honey tokens can
        be collected by Token Link and take 10 seconds to fade. Mobs that have been hit by a spike
        take less damage from other consecutive spikes.

        If Vicious Bee is Gifted, gold spikes spawn instead of normal ones. Impale will have +1
        levels of accuracy added (meaning Vicious Bee's spikes can hit mobs that are one level
        higher than it 100%). The Impale ability does more base damage when Gifted due to the +50%
        Gifted attack bonus. The attack bonus is applied to the bee itself, but it indirectly
        increases the amount of damage Impale does.[[File:Nymphimpale.png|thumb|Stick nymphs being
        attacked by a Gifted Vicious Bee's impale.]]

        Impale tokens take 24 seconds to fade away and are only generated while in combat.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Impalebackground.ogg]]

        {{clear}}

        ===Rain Cloud===
        [[File:Rain Cloud Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Rain Cloud token image]] [[File:Rain
        Cloud.png|thumb|A cloud on a field.]][[File:CloudLinkSprout.png|thumb|A cloud growing a
        sprout.]]
        [[Windy Bee]] generates '''Rain Cloud''' tokens, which summon [[Clouds]] on a different
        field. The cloud moves around the field while regrowing 49 surrounding flowers, growing
        [[Sprout]]s, and granting +25% pollen to players or +50% pollen if the player has a Gifted
        Windy Bee. The Cloud lasts for 60 seconds and increases by 5 seconds per bee's level (e.g.
        160 seconds on level 20 bee).

        When another player's Windy Bee summons a cloud on the field the player is standing in, a
        message appears, saying <span style=\"background:silver;color:white\">🌧️ {Player}'s
        Windy Bee summoned a Cloud in this field 🌧️</span>.

        Clouds can also be summoned by using [[Cloud Vial]]s, donating to the [[Wind Shrine]], and
        by [[Wild Windy Bee]].

        Rain Cloud tokens take 24 seconds to fade away and are only generated while gathering.

        There is a glitch where sometimes the Rain Cloud ability spawns a cloud in the field
        collected in.

        The following audio plays when the token is collected:[[File:Raincloud.ogg]]

        {{clear}}

        ===Tornado===
        [[File:Tornado Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Tornado token image]]
        [[Windy Bee]] can generate a '''Tornado''' token. This deletes the player's Haste stacks
        (this does not include Haste+) and summons a tornado. The tornado moves around the field
        while dealing damage to mobs, collecting pollen and tokens, and popping bubbles and fuzz
        bombs, around 5-tile-radius range (approximately 49 surrounding flower tiles) every second.
        The speed, damage, and lifespan of a summoned tornado depends on the number of Haste stacks
        spent and the level of the bee that summoned it.

        Note that if the Tornado token is collected outside or above the field, it does '''not'''
        summon a tornado but '''still''' spends Haste stacks.

        Tornado tokens take 24 seconds to fade away and are only generated by an active Windy Bee.
        The following audio plays when the tornado is active:[[File:Tornado.ogg]]

        {{Clear}}
        ===Glitch===
        [[File:Glitch Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Glitch token image]]
        [[Digital Bee]] generates a '''Glitch''' token, which corrupts the field the player is
        currently in for 20 seconds (+1s per level). A glitch effect will occasionally appear on the
        field (which can be disabled in the [[System Page]]), every ability token collected in that
        field has a chance to be [[#Ability Duplication Chance|duplicated or \"Duped\"]], and Digital
        Bee will be able to spawn [[#☺︎|☺︎ tokens]].

        The corruption on the field can be increased to raise the ability duplication chance and the
        pollen collection and instant conversion of duped abilities. The corruption can be increased
        by enhancing Digital Bee with [[Drives]]. Colored Drives increase the corruption of field
        with matching flowers while Glitched Drives increase corruption of all fields.

        Glitch tokens take 12 seconds to fade away and are only generated while gathering or during
        combat.
        [[File:Glitch.mp3.mp3|thumb|400x400px]]
        The following audio plays when the token is collected:

        {{clear}}

        ===Map Corruption===
        [[File:Map Corruption Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Map Corruption token image]]
        [[Gifted Bee|Gifted]] [[Digital Bee]] generates a '''Map Corruption''' token, which corrupts
        a random field by a small amount for 3 minutes (+10s per level), and it applies to all
        players in the server. Once the token is collected, a server-wide message announces:
        <span style=\"color:magenta;background:black;\">☺︎ [Player's display name]'s Digital Bee
        corrupted the [Field Name] ☺︎</span>.

        The amount of corruption from this can be increased by enhancing Digital Bee with
        [[Drives]]. When enhancing with drives, if the most recent drive is a colored drive, Map
        Corruption is more likely to corrupt the field with more flowers matching the color of that
        drive. And if the most recent drive is Glitched Drive, the field it chooses will be
        completely random but gains 25% more corruption.

        Map Corruption tokens take 24 seconds to fade away and are only generated while gathering.
        [[File:Map Corruption.mp3|thumb]]
        The following audio plays when the token is collected:

        {{Clear}}

        ===Mind Hack===
        [[File:Mind Hack.png|thumb|left|90px|Mind Hack token image]]
        [[Digital Bee]] generates a '''Mind Hack''' token, which hacks the minds of up to 3 nearby
        [[Mobs|enemies]] (+1 per 3 lvls), stunning them for 3 seconds (+0.1s per lvl). While hacked,
        the enemies take 25% more damage (+0.1% per [[Drives#Glitched Drive|Glitched Drive]] Digital
        Bee has been enhanced with). Hacking is only half as effective against bosses.

        This token also spawns a [[#☺︎|☺︎ token]] if collected inside the field.

        Mind Hack tokens take 16 seconds to fade away and are only generated while in combat.
        [[File:Mind Hack.mp3.mp3|thumb|400x400px]]
        The following audio plays when the token is collected:

        {{Clear}}

        ===☺===
        [[File:Smile Token.png|thumb|left|90px|☺ token image]]
        '''☺''' tokens temporarily increase field corruption, activate all [[#Ability Duplication
        Chance|duped tokens]], hack a nearby [[Mobs|non-boss enemy]] (with a similar effect as
        [[#Mind Hack|Mind Hack]]), and collect and double pollen equal to 50% of Digital Bee's
        gather amount (+50% pollen multiplier per duped token activated) in a \"☺\", \"&\", \"*\", \"~\"
        or \"?\" arrangement of flowers, with 50% of the pollen being instantly converted. The flowers
        collected by this are also stamped for 10 seconds, causing them to yield x2 pollen.

        ☺ tokens are occasionally generated by [[Digital Bee]] while inside the corrupted field, and
        the frequency for this token to be generated increases with the number of [[Drives#Glitched
        Drive|Glitched Drives]] Digital Bee has been enhanced with. Unlike most ability tokens, ☺
        tokens are always generated as duped tokens that hover above the field.

        ☺ tokens take roughly 3 seconds to fade away.

        {{Clear}}

        ==Beequip Exclusive Ability Tokens==
        ===Snowglobe Shake===
        [[File:Snowglobe Shake Token.png|thumb|left|90px|Snowglobe Shake token image]]
        '''Snowglobe Shake''' tokens are generated by bees that have the [[snowglobe]] [[beequip]]
        equipped. When collected, they give all players in the server [[Buffs & Debuffs#From
        Ability Tokens|Cool Breeze]] for 20 seconds (3%), granting x1.25 Capacity and other stats
        (+10% Convert Rate At Hive, +5% Pollen From Tools, +5% Bee Gather Pollen, +1% Honey Per
        Pollen, and +1% Loot Luck), that scale with the duration of the buff for up to 15 minutes
        (At max duration, it grants +25% Convert Rate At Hive, +25% Pollen From Tools, +25% Bee
        Gather Pollen, +5% Honey Per Pollen, and +10% Loot Luck. However, the capacity is always at
        x1.25).

        The same buff can also be obtained from using [[Snowflake]]s, which add 10 seconds of Cool
        Breeze per use. (Beesmas Only)

        Snowglobe Shake tokens take 5 seconds to fade away and are generated when gathering or when
        idle.

        {{clear}}

        ===Festive Mark===
        [[File:Festive Mark Token.png|left|thumb|90px|Festive Mark token image]]
        '''Festive Mark''' tokens are generated by [[Festive Bee]] when equipped with the [[Festive
        Wreath|festive wreath]] [[beequip]]. When collected, a festive mark is deployed in a random
        area in the field that the player is standing in. Standing within its range grants +10%
        Honey Per Pollen, x1.4 Bomb Pollen, x1.4 Red Bomb Pollen, 100% Instant Bomb Conversion, and
        5 Conversion Links. Festive Mark lasts for 12 seconds (+0.5s per lvl) and has a
        6-tile-radius range.
        [[File:Festive Mark Image.jpg|thumb|A Festive Mark on a field.]]

        Unlike other marks, Festive Mark also grants boosts to other players and its range can be
        seen by other players. It also can link with other players' marks. Despite showing (x1) when
        hovering over the buff, the Festive Mark cannot be stacked even if two overlap each other.
        When Festive Mark is active, it summons random gifts within its range that can be collected
        by any players. Rarer gifts appear more likely with bee level and certain gifts are only
        obtainable when Festive Bee is gifted. Every 4th token generated by the festive mark is
        always a Red Boost token.

        '''The gifts include:'''

        *[[#Boost|Red Boost]].
        *[[Treats]].
        *[[Strawberry]].
        *[[Pineapple]].
        *[[Blueberry]].
        *[[Sunflower Seed]].
        *[[Royal Jelly]].
        *[[Ticket]].
        *[[Gumdrops]].
        *[[Royal Jelly|Star Jelly]] (Rare).
        *[[Magic Bean]] (Rare).
        *[[Blue Extract]] (Rare).
        *[[Red Extract]] (Rare).
        *[[Glue]] (Rare).
        *[[Oil]] (Rare).
        *[[Glitter]] (Rare).
        *[[Enzymes]] (Rare).
        *[[Neonberry]] (Rare).
        *[[Field Dice]] (Rare).
        *[[Micro-Converter]] (Rare).
        *[[Gingerbread Bear]] (Very rare).
        *[[Festive Bean]] (Exceptionally rare).

        Festive Mark tokens take 16 seconds to fade away and are only generated while collecting
        pollen.

        [[Category:Mechanics]]
        [[zh-tw:技能代幣]]"}
